[[Sean Gleeson (rugby league)|Sean Gleeson]] (cousin) }} }} '''Mark Gleeson''' (born 16 July 1982) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He was contracted to [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] until the end of the [[RFL Championship|National League One]] 2008 season. Mark Gleeson was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England, and like his brother Martin, he spent seven years of his junior career in Australia after emigrating with his family aged 8, and later signed for the [[Warrington Wolves]] Academy, featuring in their 1999 under-19 Grand Final team against [[Leeds Rhinos]], he briefly returned to Australia to play in Cairns, Queensland.{{citation needed|date=January 2020}} Gleeson won Sky's [[Man of the Match]] award on full début against champions the Bradford Bulls in 2001. Gleeson re-signed for the Warrington Wolves when [[Paul Cullen (rugby league)|Paul Cullen]] took over as coach in time for the 2003's [[Super League VIII]]. He made his England début against France at Headingley in 2005.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2005/oct/24/rugbyleague.englandrugbyleagueteam|title=France serve notice of intent, but England redress balance|publisher=theguardian.com|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}} In July 2008, Mark was released from his contract at the [[Warrington Wolves]], and opted to sign for [[RFL Championship|National League One]] side [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], after his contract with [[Featherstone Rovers]] fell through.{{cite web |title=Gleeson heads for Halifax |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/3799077/gleeson-heads-for-halifax |website=Sky Sports |access-date=8 January 2026 |date=10 July 2008}} On 14 August 2008, one month after Gleeson signed for Halifax, he signed a new and improved contract until the end of the 2009 season.{{citation needed|date=January 2020}} ==Genealogical information== Mark Gleeson is the younger brother of the rugby league footballer; [[Martin Gleeson (rugby league)|Martin Gleeson]], and the cousin of the rugby league footballer; [[Sean Gleeson (rugby league)|Sean Gleeson]]. ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.warringtonwolves.org/news/Wolves7831.ink Mark Gleeson leaves]{{dead link|date=June 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }} *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20080914185332/http://site1.halifaxrlfc.co.uk/cgi-bin/newsreader.pl?item=latest-news%2F5.html Mark Gleeson signs for Halifax] {{DEFAULTSORT:Gleeson, Mark}} [[Category:1982 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Barrow Raiders players]] [[Category:English rugby league players]] [[Category:Halifax Panthers players]] [[Category:Rugby league hookers]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Wigan]] [[Category:Warrington Wolves players]] [[Category:England national rugby league team players]] {{England-rugbyleague-bio-stub}}
